# Einstein AI Connector - Full Documentation

# Configuring Agent Operations for Einstein AI Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configure the Agent Define Prompt Template Operation

Use the **Agent define prompt template** operation to define specific prompt templates for use when
integrating with the LLM of your choice. This allows you to create natural language prompts that
generate responses, extract information, invoke other prompts, or perform any text-based task.

1.  Select the operation on the Anypoint Code Builder or Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab for the operation, enter these values:
    - **Template**

      Enter the prompt template for the operation.

    - **Instructions**

      Provide the instructions for the LLM, outlining the goals of the task.

    - **Dataset**

      Specify the dataset for the LLM to evaluate using the provided template and instructions.

3.  In **Additional properties**, enter these values:
    - **Model name**

      Select the model name. The default is `OpenAI GPT 3.5 Turbo`.

    - **Probability**

      Enter the probability of the model staying accurate. The default is `0.8`. **Locale**

      Enter the localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and
      expected output locale(s). The default is `en_US`.

This is the XML for this operation:

```xml
<ms-einstein-ai:agent-define-prompt-template
  doc:name="Agent define prompt template"
  doc:id="f1c29c39-eac9-468c-9c46-4109a66303ec"
  config-ref="Einstein_AI"
  template="#[payload.template]"
  instructions="#[payload.instructions]"
  dataset="#[payload.dataset]"
/>
```

### Output Configuration

This operation responds with a JSON payload containing the main LLM response. Additionally,
attributes such as token usage are included as part of the metadata (attributes) but not within the
main payload. This additional metadata is particularly useful to track token usage across
applications and manage costs associated with LLM adoption.

This is an example response:

```json
{
  "response": "{\n  \"type\": \"positive\",\n  \"response\": \"Thank you for your positive feedback on the training last week. We are glad to hear that you had a great experience. Have a nice day!\"\n}"
}
```

The operation also returns attributes that arenât within the main JSON payload, which include
information about token usage, for example:

```json
{
  "tokenUsage": {
    "outputCount": 9,
    "totalCount": 18,
    "inputCount": 9
  },
  "additionalAttributes": {}
}
```

- `tokenUsage`

  Token usage metadata returned as attributes

- `outputCount`

  Number of tokens used to generate the output

- `totalCount`

  Total number of tokens used for input and output

- `inputCount`

  Number of tokens used to process the input

## See Also

- [Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-acb)
- [Using Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Einstein AI Connector Reference](einstein-ai-connector-reference)
- [Einstein AI - Additional Configuration Information](einstein-ai-additional-config)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Configuring Chat Operations for Einstein AI Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configure the Chat Answer Prompt Operation

The **Chat answer prompt** operation sends a request to the configured LLM. This operation uses a
plain text prompt as input and responds with a plain text answer.

1.  Select the operation on the Anypoint Code Builder or Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab for the operation, enter plain text for the **Prompt**.
3.  In **Additional properties**, enter these values:
    - **Model name**

      Select the model name. The default is `OpenAI GPT 3.5 Turbo`.

    - **Probability**

      Enter the probability of the model staying accurate. The default is `0.8`. **Locale**

      Enter the localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and
      expected output locale(s). The default is `en_US`.

This is the XML configuration for this operation:

```xml
<ms-einstein-ai:chat-answer-prompt
  doc:name="Chat answer prompt"
  doc:id="66426c0e-5626-4dfa-88ef-b09f77577261"
  config-ref="Einstein_AI"
  prompt="#[payload.prompt]"
/>
```

## Configure the Chat Generate from Messages Operation

The **Chat generate from messages** operation is a prompt request operation to the configured LLM,
with provided messages. This operation accepts multiple messages and uses a plain text prompt as
input. The operation responds with a plain text answer.

1.  Select the operation on the Anypoint Code Builder or Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab for the operation, enter plain text for the **Messages**.
3.  In **Additional properties**, enter these values:
    - **Model name**

      Select the model name. The default is `OpenAI GPT 3.5 Turbo`.

    - **Probability**

      Enter the probability of the model staying accurate. The default is `0.8`. **Locale**

      Enter the localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and
      expected output locale(s). The default is `en_US`.

This is the configuration XML for this operation:

```xml
<ms-einstein-ai:chat-generate-from-messages
  doc:name="Chat generate from messages"
  doc:id="94fa27f3-18ce-436c-8a5f-10b8dbfa4ea3"
  config-ref="Einstein_AI"
  messages="#[payload.messages]"
/>
```

## See Also

- [Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-acb)
- [Einstein AI Connector Overview](./)
- [Einstein AI Connector Reference](einstein-ai-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Configuring Embedding Operations for Einstein AI Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configure the Adhoc File Query Operation

The **Embedding Adhoc File Query** operation takes a document and ingests it into the vector
database along with its query.

The output of this operation is a set of scores with the complete content of the document thatâs the
most likely answer to the query. The vector database is used to identify the numeric representation
of the content before creating the likely score.

1.  Select the operation on the Anypoint Code Builder or Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab for the operation, enter these values:
    - **Prompt**

      Plain text for the prompt to send to the LLM

    - **File path**

      Full file path for the document to ingest into the embedding store. Ensure the file path is
      accessible.

      You can also use a DataWeave expression for this field, for example:

      ```dataweave
      mule.home ++ "/apps/" ++ app.name ++ "/customer-service.pdf"
      ```

3.  In **Additional Properties**, select:
    - **Model name**

      Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.

    - **File type**
      - **Text**

        Any type of text files (JSON, XML, txt, CSV, and so on)

      - **PDF**

        System-generated PDF files

      - **CSV**

        CSV file with comma-separated values

      - **URL**

        A single URL.

    - **Option type**

      How to split the document prior to ingestion into the vector database

## Configure the Generate From File Operation

The **Embedding generate from file** operation takes a document and ingests it into the vector
database. The output of this operation is a numeric representation of the content.

1.  Select the operation on the Anypoint Code Builder or Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab for the operation, enter these values:
    - **File path**

      Full file path for the document to ingest into the embedding store. Ensure the file path is
      accessible.

      You can also use a DataWeave expression for this field, for example:

      ```dataweave
      mule.home ++ "/apps/" ++ app.name ++ "/customer-service.pdf"
      ```

3.  In **Additional Properties**, select:
    - **Model name**

      Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.

    - **File type**
      - **Text**

        Any type of text files (JSON, XML, txt, CSV, and so on)

      - **PDF**

        System-generated PDF files

      - **CSV**

        CSV file with comma-separated values

      - **URL**

        A single URL.

    - **Option type**

      How to split the document prior to ingestion into the vector database

### How Data is Parsed

Data from files are parsed in a way that changes the format of the generated embeddings slightly
(the content itself isnât changed):

- Spaces are removed from the beginning and end of file content, for example:

  Before parsing:

  ```txt
  "

  Para 1
  Para 2



  "
  ```

  After parsing:

  ```txt
  "Para 1
  Para 2"
  ```

- Extra lines between paragraphs are removed, for example:

  Before parsing:

  ```txt
  "
  Para 1


  Para2
  "
  ```

  After parsing:

  ```txt
  "Para 1
  Para 2"
  ```

- The connector provides filtering logic that removes some characters from the generated embedding,
  for example, non-breaking spaces (<0xa0>). These characters are removed to drive more accurate
  embeddings:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Character</th><th>Hex Code</th><th>Decimal Code</th><th>Name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>\u0000</p></td><td><p>0x00</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td><p>Null (NULL)</p></td><td><p>Marks the end of a string in C-like languages</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>\u0001</p></td><td><p>0x01</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td><p>Start of Heading (SOH)</p></td><td><p>Used to mark the beginning of a heading in data streams</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>\u0007</p></td><td><p>0x07</p></td><td><p>7</p></td><td><p>Bell (BEL)</p></td><td><p>Triggers a beep or alert sound</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>\u0008</p></td><td><p>0x08</p></td><td><p>8</p></td><td><p>Backspace (BS)</p></td><td><p>Moves the cursor one position backward</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>\u0009</p></td><td><p>0x09</p></td><td><p>9</p></td><td><p>Horizontal Tab (TAB)</p></td><td><p>Inserts a tab space</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>\u000A</p></td><td><p>0x0A</p></td><td><p>10</p></td><td><p>Line Feed (LF)</p></td><td><p>Moves to a new line (Unix newline)</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>\u000D</p></td><td><p>0x0D</p></td><td><p>13</p></td><td><p>Carriage Return (CR)</p></td><td><p>Returns to the beginning of a new line</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>\u001B</p></td><td><p>0x1B</p></td><td><p>27</p></td><td><p>Escape (ESC)</p></td><td><p>Used to introduce escape sequences for control</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>\u001F</p></td><td><p>0x1F</p></td><td><p>31</p></td><td><p>Unit Separator (US)</p></td><td><p>Separates units of information</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure the Generate From Text Operation

The **Embedding generate from text** operation takes text and ingests it into the vector database.
The output of this operation is a numeric representation of the content.

1.  Select the operation on the Anypoint Code Builder or Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab for the operation, enter these values:
    - **Text**

      Text to ingest into the vector database

3.  In **Additional Properties**, select:
    - **Model name**

      Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM

## See Also

- [Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-acb)
- [Using Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Einstein AI Connector Overview](./)
- [Einstein AI Connector Reference](einstein-ai-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Configuring RAG Operations for Einstein AI Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a technique for enhancing AI-generated outputs by retrieving
relevant content, and using it to augment AI prompts with additional context. By grounding LLMs with
this additional information, they can provide more accurate and reliable responses.

## Configure the RAG Adhoc Load Document Operation

The **RAG adhoc load document** operation retrieves information based on a plain text prompt from an
in-memory embedding store.

To configure the **RAG adhoc load document** operation:

1.  Select the operation on the Anypoint Code Builder or Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab for the operation, enter these values:
    - **Prompt**

      The prompt to send to the LLM and the embedding store to respond to.

    - **File Path**

      Contains the full file path for the document to ingest into the embedding store. Ensure the
      file path is accessible.

      You can also use a DataWeave expression for this field, for example:

      `mule.home "/apps/" app.name ++ "/customer-service.pdf"`

3.  In **Additional properties**, select the values for:
    - **Embedding name**
    - **File type**

      Type of document to ingest into the embedding store:
      - **Text**
      - **PDF**
      - **CSV**
      - URL

        A single URL pointing to web content to ingest.

    - **Option type**

      How to split the document prior to ingestion into the vector database

    - **Model name**

      Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.

    - **Probability**

      Probability of the model API staying accurate

    - **Locale**

      Localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and expected
      output locales

This is the XML configuration for this operation:

```xml
<ms-einstein-ai:rag-adhoc-load-document
  doc:name="Rag adhoc load document"
  doc:id="edaea124-a8aa-4d4a-8f85-0f32ee4c9858"
  config-ref="Einstein_AI"
  prompt="#[payload.prompt]"
  filePath="#[payload.filePath]"
  optionType="PARAGRAPH"
/>
```

## See Also

- [Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-acb)
- [Using Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Einstein AI Connector Overview](./)
- [Einstein AI Connector Reference](einstein-ai-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Configuring Tools Operations for Einstein AI Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use the **Tools use AI service** operation to create autonomous agents that can use external tools
whenever a prompt canât be answered directly by the AI model.

## Before You Begin

To use Tools operations, you must create a `tools.config.json` file that includes all the necessary
API information for the operation to execute APIs successfully, for example:

```json
[
  {
    "action": "Execute POST requests for API endpoints.",
    "url": "http://check-inventory-material.cloudhub.io/mmbe",
    "name": "Check Inventory",
    "method": "POST",
    "headers": "Basic XXX",
    "example-payload": "{\n \"materialNo\": \"paramValue\"}",
    "query": [
      "Check inventory in SAP",
      "Check Stock Overview",
      "Show product availability for MULETEST0",
      "Check the inventory for MULETEST0",
      "Check Stock for 400-110"
    ],
    "description": "Check inventory details for material in SAP by providing the materialNo as input for paramValue. Please use the materialNo and not materialNumber. This action applies whenever users' intent is 'Stock overview', 'product availability', 'Inventory', 'available stock'. Use the headers to perform the request."
  },
  {
    "action": "Execute GET requests for API endpoints.",
    "url": "https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/mocking/api/v1/sources/exchange/assets/612eb739-4266-4f4c-bf2f-29953c153d80/accounts-api/1.0.1/m/accounts",
    "name": "Show Accounts",
    "method": "GET",
    "headers": "",
    "example-payload": "{}",
    "query": ["Get all accounts", "Show all accounts from CRM"],
    "description": "Get all accounts from a CRM. This action applies whenever users' intent is 'CRM accounts', 'customers', 'customer accounts', 'accounts'."
  },
  {
    "action": "Execute GET requests for API endpoints.",
    "url": "https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/mocking/api/v1/sources/exchange/assets/7b99cead-a984-497b-9e6c-c16a3b4dcb76/employee-api/1.0.1/m/employees",
    "name": "Show Employees",
    "method": "GET",
    "headers": "",
    "example-payload": "{}",
    "query": [
      "Get all employee information",
      "Show employees for the country Switzerland",
      "How many employees do we have",
      "Show a list of all employees"
    ],
    "description": "Get all information about employees. This action applies whenever users' intent is 'employees', 'workforce'."
  }
]
```

Each object in the tools array represents a tool that the autonomous agent can use, so it is
important to describe each field as accurately as possible.

- `action`

  This field supports the following options:
  - `Execute GET` requests for API endpoints.
  - `Execute POST` requests for API endpoints.

- `url`

  The complete URL to the endpoint, including the resource path.

- `name`

  The name of the action in natural language.

- `method`

  The HTTP method for the endpoint. Only POST and GET are supported.

- `headers`

  The HTTP headers required for authorization to the endpoint. Only Basic Auth is supported.

- `example-payload`

  The example payload in JSON format.

- `query`

  Example queries a user might use to invoke this action.

- `description`

  A detailed description of how to use the tool. The description should be comprehensive enough for
  the LLM to understand its purpose and usage.

Ensure that the file path to the `tools.config.json` is accessible to the application at runtime.

## Configure the Tools Use AI Service Operation

To configure the **Tools use AI service** operation:

1.  Select the operation on the Anypoint Code Builder or Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab for the operation, enter these values:
    - **Prompt**

      The prompt to send to the LLM.

    - **Input Stream**

### Output Configuration

This operation responds with a JSON payload that contains the main LLM response. Additionally,
attributes such as token usage and tool usage (boolean value) are included as part of the
attributes, but not within the main payload.

This is an example response of the JSON payload if the prompt is "What is the capital of
Switzerland?":

```json
{
  "response": "The capital of Switzerland is Bern."
}
```

If the prompt requires accessing external data through a tool, for example, "Show me the latest
account created in Salesforce", the AI model uses the configured tool to fetch the information,
resulting in:

```json
{
  "response": "The latest account created in Salesforce is:\n\n- **Name:** MuleTalks\n- **Id:** 0010600002JKf42AAD\n- **Created Date:** 2024-07-10T20:21:36.000Z\n\nIf you have any further questions or need additional assistance, feel free to ask!"
}
```

Along with the JSON payload, the operation returns attributes, which include information about token
and tool usage, for example:

```json
{
    "tokenUsage": { (1)
        "outputCount": 9,
        "totalCount": 18,
        "inputCount": 9
    },
    "additionalAttributes": { (2)
        "toolsUsed": "true"
    }
}
```

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>tokenUsage: Provides information on the token usage for the operation:<div><ul><li><p><code>outputCount</code></p><div><p>Number of tokens generated in the response</p></div></li><li><p><code>totalCount</code></p><div><p>Total number of tokens processed for the entire operation, including input and output</p></div></li><li><p><code>inputCount</code></p><div><p>Number of tokens processed from the input query or document</p></div></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td><code>additionalAttributes</code> includes the boolean value for <code>toolsUsed</code>:<div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>External tools, such as APIs or integrations, were used to gather or generate the response.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>No external tools were used, and the response was based on internal knowledge only.</p></div></li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-acb)
- [Using Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Einstein AI Connector Overview](./)
- [Einstein AI Connector Reference](einstein-ai-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# Einstein AI Connector 1.2 - Additional Configuration Information

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Einstein AI Connector supports these operations, which are categorized into:

- [Agent](configuring-agent-operations)
- [Chat](configuring-chat-operations)
- [Embeddings](configuring-embeddings-operations)
- [RAG](configuring-rag-operations)
- [Tools](configuring-tools-operations)
---

# Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure Einstein AI Connector 1.2

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

**Open Beta Release**: The **cloud IDE is in open beta**. Any use of Anypoint Code Builder in its
beta state is subject to the applicable beta services terms and conditions, available from the IDE.

To use Anypoint Code Builder to configure a connector or module, create a basic integration project
in Anypoint Code Builder, add connectors and other components to your Mule application to process
your business logic, and configure the attributes.

## Before You Begin

Before creating an integration project, you must:

- [Set up and access the Anypoint Code Builder web or desktop IDE](../../anypoint-code-builder/start-acb)
- Have credentials to access the connectorâs API
- Ensure that the Visual Studio Code Extension Pack for Java is installed.

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)

## Create a New Integration Project

To create a new integration project:

1.  In the activity bar of the IDE, click the
    ![Anypoint Code Builder icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-icon-ms-logo.png) (**Anypoint Code
    Builder**) icon.
2.  From **Quick Actions**, click **Develop an Integration**:

    ![Develop an Integration link highlighted in the Getting started section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/develop-integration-option-mat.png)

3.  Complete the following in the **Develop an Integration** form:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Name</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique name for your project.</p></div><div><p>This name is used as the title and name of the integration project file. For example, if the project name is "Salesforce Integration," the project file name is <code>salesforce-integration</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Location</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your home directory or another directory you create. Click <strong>Browse</strong> to select a different directory for the integration project.</p></div><div><p>Donât create the project within another project directory.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="../../anypoint-code-builder/start-add-folders">Adding Folders to Your Home Directory</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Select the type of project to create by selecting either the **Empty Project** or **Template or
    Example** card.
5.  Select the **Mule runtime** and **Java Version** for your app.
6.  Click **Create Project**.

## Add Components to Your Project

Add components to your integration project to build a flow:

1.  In the **Explorer** view, open the XML file for your project, such as `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Select **Build a Flow** from the start card to create an empty flow:

    ![Anypoint Code Builder canvas with starting cards with the options to start from scratch, build a flow, or build a subflow](../../connectors/_images/acb-build-flow.png)

3.  Change the name of the flow:
    1.  Click **Flow**.
    2.  Click the edit icon.

        ![Arrow pointing to the flow name edit pencil icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-change-flow-name.png)

    3.  Enter the flow name, and click the checkmark.
    4.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    5.  In the **Add Component** panel, search for and select your component from the results. The
        following example shows the **Listener** component from the **HTTP** search results:

        ![Listener component highlighted in the Add Component section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/main-tutorial-add-first-listener.png)

        The configuration XML file now includes the XML for the HTTP Listener into the `<flow/>`
        element, for example:

        ```XML
        <flow name="getFlights" >
          <http:listener path="" config-ref="" doc:name="Listener" doc:id="ojzfry" />
        </flow>
        ```

## Configure the Trigger For the Flow

A trigger (source) starts the flow when specified conditions are met. If a connector has its own
associated sources, you can use a connector-specific trigger to initiate the flow. Each connector
trigger has a unique configuration.

You can configure the following sources for this connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener trigger, follow these steps:

1.  Click the **Listener** component on the canvas UI.
2.  (Optional) Click the edit icon to change the name of the **Listener** component.
3.  (Optional) If you created a configuration file for the connector, select it.
4.  In the **General** tab, in the **Path** field, enter the relative path for the path set in the
    HTTP configuration element. See
    [Configure Paths](../../http-connector/latest/http-listener-ref#configure-paths).
5.  Select the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Allowed methods</p></td><td><p>To allow all HTTP methods (default), do not define this attribute. To restrict certain methods, specify all allowed HTTP methods, for example, <code>GET, POST</code>, as a comma-separated list.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response streaming mode</p></td><td><p>Whether to use streaming when the response is sent.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output MIME type</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameters</p></td><td><p>Appends a key-value pair for a reader property to the value of the Output MIME attribute. Multiple key-value pairs are allowed.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output encoding</p></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><p>Configures the way data streams are consumed in Mule apps.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary node only</p></td><td><p>Select this option to execute the <strong>Listener</strong> trigger on the primary node only when running in a cluster.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery policy</p></td><td><p>Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a redelivery policy to any source in a flow.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response</p></td><td><p>See <a href="../../http-connector/latest/http-documentation#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Response</p></td><td><p>HTTP error response returns a status code for the error.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><p>Body of the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><p>Headers to include in the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Status code</p></td><td><p>Status code of the error response, for example, <code>500</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason phrase</p></td><td><p>Reason for the error, for example, <code>Gateway Timeout</code> or <code>Internal Server Error</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add the Connector to Your Integration Project

1.  In the Explorer view, open the configuration XML file for your project, such as
    `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Click the
    ![Show Mule graphical mode icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-tree-view.png) (**Show
    Mule graphical mode**) icon in the activity bar to open the canvas UI if it doesnât open
    automatically.
3.  Add the connector the same way you added other components from the canvas UI:
    1.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    2.  In the **Add Component** panel, click **Connectors**.

4.  Click the
    ![Search in Exchange icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-search-exchange.png)
    (**Search in Exchange**) toggle:

    ![Search in Exchange toggle](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-connector-search-exchange.png)

5.  Enter `einstein ai` in the search box.
6.  Select the connector to add it to your project.

Adding a connector to an integration project doesnât make that connector available to other projects
in your Anypoint Code Builder workspace.

## Add a Global Configuration

1.  In the configuration XML, place your cursor before the `<flow/>` element and type `einstein-ai`.

    Ensure that the cursor is not inside the `<flow/>` element.

2.  Select the `ms-einstein-ai:config` snippet:

    The snippet adds the following code:

    ```xml
    <ms-einstein-ai:config llmType="#[]" configType="#[]" filePath="#[]" modelName="#[]" name="name" />
    ```

3.  Add the attributes to complete the configuration, for example:

    ```xml
    <ms-einstein-ai:config name="Einstein_AI_Config1" doc:name="Einstein AI Config" doc:id="96f110e3-f17c-4405-8911-80345985da12" >
    	<ms-einstein-ai:oauth-client-credentials-connection >
    			<ms-einstein-ai:oauth-client-credentials clientId="myclientid" clientSecret="myclientsecret" />
    	</ms-einstein-ai:oauth-client-credentials-connection>
    </ms-einstein-ai:config>
    ```

    This example shows a flow named "Define_Prompt" with two global elements defined
    (`http:listener-config` and `ms-einstein-ai:config`):

    ```xml
    <http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="0868b5a9-90e8-4470-a255-8b9e8e3938a7" >
    	<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
    </http:listener-config>
    <ms-einstein-ai:config name="Einstein_AI_Config" doc:name="Einstein AI Config" doc:id="a68f8f2c-a560-4d8e-9ebe-98a7d8d716cd" >
    	<ms-einstein-ai:oauth-client-credentials-connection >
    			<ms-einstein-ai:oauth-client-credentials clientId="myclientid" clientSecret="myclientsecret" />
    	</ms-einstein-ai:oauth-client-credentials-connection>
    </ms-einstein-ai:config>
    <flow name="Define_Prompt">
    ```

## Example Customer Feedback Flow

This example flow is designed to handle customer feedback analysis and responses using the Einstein
AI service.

In the first route of the scatter-gather component, the flow defines a prompt template for an agent
to analyze customer feedback provided in a dataset. If the feedback is negative, the agent is
instructed to open a service case and apologize to the customer.

In the second route of the scatter-gather component, the flow defines a chat answer prompt for
interacting with the Einstein AI service.

![A flow with a Tranform component, a scatter-gather component, and two routes](_images/einstein-ai-example-flow-acb.png)

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for the connector, configure the other required fields. The
required fields vary depending on which connector actions you use.

For more information about configuring Einstein AI Connector actions, see
[Einstein AI Connector - Additional Configuration Information](einstein-ai-additional-config).

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Einstein AI Connector Reference](einstein-ai-connector-reference)
- [Einstein AI - Additional Configuration Information](einstein-ai-additional-config)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Einstein AI Connector 1.2 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Einstein AI Connector provides access to the Salesforce Einstein Trust Layer through the Models
APIs.

## Configurations

---

### Config

Default configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_Connection">Connection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Connection

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#DefaultHttpProxyConfig">Default Http Proxy Config</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of outbound connections to keep open at the same time.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of time to wait when initially establishing the TCP connection between the connector and Salesforce Agent before throwing an exception if the connection fails.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of units that a connection can remain idle before it is closed.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Protocol to use for communication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth public client ID as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth client secret that corresponds to the client ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>https://{salesforceorg}/services/oauth2/token/</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scopes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not provided, it defaults the scopes in the annotation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule runtime automatically provisions the default one.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

- [AGENT Define Prompt Template](#AgentDefinePromptTemplate)
- [CHAT Answer Prompt](#ChatAnswerPrompt)
- [CHAT Generate From Messages](#ChatGenerateFromMessages)
- [EMBEDDING Adhoc File Query](#EmbeddingAdhocFileQuery)
- [EMBEDDING Generate From File](#EmbeddingGenerateFromFile)

- [RAG Adhoc Load Document](#RagAdhocLoadDocument)
- [Tools Use Ai Service](#ToolsUseAiService)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)

## AGENT Define Prompt Template

`<ms-einstein-ai:agent-define-prompt-template>`

Use the **Agent define prompt template** operation to use specific prompt templates. The prompt
template language is a simple way to define and compose AI functions using plain text. You can use
it to create natural language prompts, generate responses, extract information, invoke other
prompts, or perform any other task that can be expressed with text.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Template</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Prompt template for the operation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Instructions</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Instructions for the LLM, outlining the goals of the task.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Dataset</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Dataset for the LLM to evaluate using the provided template and instructions.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Model API Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>sfdc_ai__DefaultOpenAIGPT35Turbo</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Probability</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Probability of the model API staying accurate.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0.8</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Locale</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and expected output locale(s).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>en_US</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#EinsteinResponseAttributes">Einstein Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:CHAT_FAILURE
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## CHAT Answer Prompt

`<ms-einstein-ai:chat-answer-prompt>`

The **Chat answer prompt** operation sends a prompt request to the configured LLM. It uses a plain
text prompt as input and responds with a plain text answer.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prompt</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Plain text prompt to send to the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Model API Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>sfdc_ai__DefaultOpenAIGPT35Turbo</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Probability</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Probability of the model API staying accurate.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0.8</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Locale</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and expected output locale(s).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>en_US</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#EinsteinResponseAttributes">Einstein Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:CHAT_FAILURE
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## CHAT Generate From Messages

`<ms-einstein-ai:chat-generate-from-messages>`

The **Chat generate from messages** operation is a prompt request operation with provided messages
to the configured LLM. It uses a plain text prompt as input and responds with a plain text answer.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Messages</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Model API Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>sfdc_ai__DefaultOpenAIGPT35Turbo</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Probability</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Probability of the model API staying accurate.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0.8</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Locale</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and expected output locale(s).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>en_US</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ResponseParameters">Response Parameters</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:CHAT_FAILURE
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## EMBEDDING Adhoc File Query

`<ms-einstein-ai:embedding-adhoc-file-query>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prompt</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Plain text prompt to send to the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Stream</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Model API Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>sfdc_ai__DefaultOpenAITextEmbeddingAda_002</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of the document to ingest into the embedding store:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Text</p></li><li><p>PDF</p></li><li><p>CSV</p></li><li><p>URL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>PDF</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Option Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to split the document prior to ingestion into the embedding or vector database.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>FULL</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Option Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>FULL</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:EMBEDDING_OPERATIONS_FAILURE
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:RAG_FAILURE
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:TOOLS_OPERATION_FAILURE

## EMBEDDING Generate From File

`<ms-einstein-ai:embedding-generate-from-file>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Stream</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## EMBEDDING Generate From Text

`<ms-einstein-ai:embedding-generate-from-text>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Text</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Model API Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>sfdc_ai__DefaultOpenAITextEmbeddingAda_002</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ResponseParameters">Response Parameters</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Config](#Config)

### Throws

- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:CONNECTIVITY
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:EMBEDDING_OPERATIONS_FAILURE
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:RAG_FAILURE
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- MS-EINSTEIN-AI:TOOLS_OPERATION_FAILURE

## RAG Adhoc Load Document

`<ms-einstein-ai:rag-adhoc-load-document>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prompt</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Plain text prompt to send to the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Full file path for the document to ingest into the embedding store. Ensure the file path is accessible.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Embedding Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>sfdc_ai__DefaultOpenAITextEmbeddingAda_002</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>File Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of the document to ingest into the embedding store:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Text</p></li><li><p>PDF</p></li><li><p>CSV</p></li><li><p>URL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>PDF</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Option Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to split the document prior to ingestion into the embedding or vector database.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>FULL</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Model API Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>sfdc_ai__DefaultOpenAIGPT35Turbo</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Probability</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Probability of the model API staying accurate.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0.8</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Locale</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and expected output locale(s).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>en_US</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Tools Use Ai Service

`<ms-einstein-ai:tools-use-ai-service>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prompt</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Plain text prompt to send to the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Input Stream</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>x</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Model API Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the API model that interacts with the LLM.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>sfdc_ai__DefaultOpenAIGPT35Turbo</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Probability</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Probability of the model API staying accurate.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0.8</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Locale</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Localization information, which can include the default locale, input locale(s), and expected output locale(s).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>en_US</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read timeout value.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Error Mappings</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Set of error mappings</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
